# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Q1. **[KTU University Exam – May 2024, Set B]**
*Define a robotic circuit. List its main components with one-line functions.*

**Model Answer:**

> A **robotic circuit** is an integrated fluid-power and electronic system that drives and controls the joints of an industrial robot. Main components:
> 1. **Power source** — Motor-pump unit (hydraulic) or compressor (pneumatic) generating pressurised fluid.
> 2. **Control valves** — DCV / proportional / servo valves that meter flow and direction.
> 3. **Actuators** — Cylinders or motors converting fluid energy to mechanical motion.
> 4. **Sensors** — LVDT, encoder, pressure transmitter providing closed-loop feedback.
> 5. **Controller** — PLC / CNC issuing command signals to valve solenoids.
> 6. **Reservoir and conditioning** — Tank, filter, cooler, accumulator maintaining fluid quality.

*(3 marks — full marks if all six points are listed with functions.)*

---

### Q2. **[KTU University Exam – Dec 2023]**
*Differentiate between an open-loop and closed-loop robotic circuit. Give one example each.*

**Model Answer:**

| Aspect | Open-Loop | Closed-Loop |
|---|---|---|
| Feedback | Absent | Position / pressure / force fed back |
| Accuracy | Low (depends on valve repeatability) | High (error-corrected) |
| Cost | Low | High (sensors + controller) |
| Example | Pneumatic pick-and-place with reed-switch end stops | Hydraulic servo welding robot with LVDT joint feedback |

*(3 marks.)*

---

## 5.2 Part B — Descriptive / Numerical (14 Marks Each, Internal Choice)

> **INTERNAL CHOICE:** Answer **either** Question A **or** Question B in full.

---

### Question A — Option 1 (14 Marks)

#### (a) **[7 Marks]** With a neat circuit diagram, explain the working of a **hydraulic servo system** used in a robotic joint. Derive the open-loop transfer function.

**Model Solution Outline:**

1. **Circuit description (3 marks):** Draw a 4/3 servo valve feeding a double-acting cylinder. Identify supply, return, pilot, and drain lines. Show LVDT feedback to the servo amplifier.

2. **Linearised valve equation (2 marks):**

   $$Q_L = K_q x_v - K_c P_L$$

3. **Continuity + force balance (1 mark):** Write both equations as in Section 3.2 of these notes.

4. **Final transfer function (1 mark):** Write the boxed $\dfrac{X(s)}{X_v(s)}$ expression and state the hydraulic natural frequency.

#### (b) **[7 Marks]** A robotic joint cylinder has piston area $A_p = 30 \, cm^2$, annular area $A_2 = 20 \, cm^2$, stroke = 0.4 m, load mass $M = 80 \, kg$. The pump supplies oil at $P_s = 200$ bar with a flow of 6 L/min. Compute (i) extension force, (ii) extension velocity, (iii) hydraulic power required. Assume return pressure = 5 bar, no friction.

**Step-by-step solution:**

**(i) Extension force**

$$
\begin{aligned}
F_{ext} &= P_s \cdot A_p - P_{ret} \cdot A_2 \\[4pt]
&= (200 \times 10^5)(30 \times 10^{-4}) - (5 \times 10^5)(20 \times 10^{-4}) \\[4pt]
&= 60000 - 1000 = 59000 \, N
\end{aligned}
$$

**(ii) Extension velocity**

$$
\begin{aligned}
v &= \dfrac{Q}{A_p} = \dfrac{6 \times 10^{-3} / 60}{30 \times 10^{-4}} = \dfrac{1 \times 10^{-4}}{3 \times 10^{-3}} = 0.0333 \, m/s
\end{aligned}
$$

**(iii) Hydraulic power**

$$
\begin{aligned}
P_{hyd} &= (P_s - P_{ret}) \cdot Q = (200 - 5)\times 10^5 \cdot (1 \times 10^{-4}) = 1950 \, W \approx 1.95 \, kW
\end{aligned}
$$

> **Final answer:** $F_{ext} = 59 \, kN$, $v = 0.0333 \, m/s$, $P_{hyd} = 1.95 \, kW$. *(Full 7 marks.)*

---

### Question B — Option 2 (14 Marks)

#### (a) **[7 Marks]** Explain the **electro-pneumatic sequencing circuit** for a 2-axis pick-and-place robot. Draw the pneumatic circuit and the PLC ladder diagram.

**Model Solution Outline:**

1. **Pneumatic circuit (3 marks):** Show FRL → two 5/3 DCVs (one per axis) → two cylinders. Show reed-switch feedback to PLC inputs.

2. **PLC ladder (3 marks):** Write 4 rungs corresponding to the four motion states (A+, B+, A−, B−) using latching relays (SET/RST or OTL/OTU instructions). Cross-reference limit switches $RA, RB, LA, LB$.

3. **Sequence explanation (1 mark):** State the operational logic (Idle → A+ → B+ → A− → B− → Idle) with the conditions that cause each transition.

#### (b) **[7 Marks]** A double-acting cylinder drives a robot gripper. The cap-end area is $25 \, cm^2$, rod-end area is $15 \, cm^2$. The supply pressure is 80 bar, return pressure is 4 bar. The grip force required on the workpiece is 200 N per jaw (2 jaws). Determine the maximum allowable payload mass the cylinder can lift in pure vertical motion. Assume frictionless guide.

**Solution:**

**Step 1 — Available extension force:**

$$
\begin{aligned}
F_{ext} &= (80 \times 10^5)(25 \times 10^{-4}) - (4 \times 10^5)(15 \times 10^{-4}) \\[4pt]
&= 20000 - 600 = 19400 \, N
\end{aligned}
$$

**Step 2 — Reserve for gripping:** Each jaw requires 200 N → 2 jaws → $F_{grip} = 400 \, N$.

**Step 3 — Net force available for payload lift:**

$$
\begin{aligned}
F_{lift} &= 19400 - 400 = 19000 \, N
\end{aligned}
$$

**Step 4 — Maximum payload mass:**

$$
\begin{aligned}
m_{max} &= \dfrac{F_{lift}}{g} = \dfrac{19000}{9.81} \approx 1937 \, kg
\end{aligned}
$$

> **Final answer:** $m_{max} \approx 1.94 \times 10^3 \, kg$. *(Full 7 marks.)*

---

## 5.3 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning

> [!WARNING]
> **Common Pitfalls — Where Students Lose Marks**
> 1. **Unit mismatch in $Q$.** The examiner deducts 1 mark if $Q$ is in L/min instead of m³/s in the final power calculation. Always convert: $1 \, L/min = 1.667 \times 10^{-5} \, m^3/s$.
> 2. **Confusing cap-end and rod-end areas.** In *retraction*, the roles of $A_1$ and $A_2$ swap. Read the question carefully: extension vs. retraction.
> 3. **Skipping the linearisation step.** A servo-valve equation is only valid for *small* spool displacements. If asked for transfer function, you MUST write $Q_L = K_q x_v - K_c P_L$ before Laplace-transforming. Direct Laplace of the non-linear orifice equation = **0 marks** for the derivation.
> 4. **Forgetting $\omega_h$ in the final answer.** A transfer function without the natural-frequency substitution is incomplete; the examiner expects both $\omega_h$ and $\zeta_h$.
> 5. **No unit on final numerical answer.** Always append $\, m/s, \, N, \, rad/s$ etc. A bare number = -0.5 mark.

---

## 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Rapid-Revision Checklist for Robotic Circuits (Module 4)**
> - **Definition:** Robotic circuit = power pack + control valve + actuator + sensor + controller loop.
> - **Three flavours:** Hydraulic (heavy), Pneumatic (light/clean), Electro-hydraulic servo (precision).
> - **Key force equation:** $F_{ext} = P_1 A_1 - P_2 A_2$ (extension), swap $A_1 \leftrightarrow A_2$ for retraction.
> - **Key velocity equation:** $v = Q / A_1$.
> - **Key power equation:** $P_{hyd} = \Delta P \cdot Q$.
> - **Servo valve linearised:** $Q_L = K_q x_v - K_c P_L$.
> - **Continuity equation:** $Q_L = A_p s X(s) + \dfrac{V_t}{4 \beta_e} s P_L(s)$.
> - **Force balance:** $A_p P_L = M s^2 X(s) + B s X(s) + K X(s)$.
> - **Hydraulic natural frequency:** $\omega_h = \sqrt{4 \beta_e A_p^2 / (V_t M)}$ — must be **≥ 30 Hz** for industrial robots.
> - **Damping ratio:** $\zeta_h = (K_{ce}/2A_p) \sqrt{\beta_e M / V_t}$ — target **0.2 – 0.7** for stable response.
> - **Sequencing logic:** Use PLC ladder or pneumatic cascade valves; verify with limit-switch feedback.
> - **Common exam trap:** Bulk modulus $\beta_e$ in **Pa** (not bar); $1 \, bar = 10^5 \, Pa$.
> - **Always** state assumptions: frictionless, leak-free, linearised valve, isothermal oil.
> - **Practical applications:** welding, forging, palletizing, pick-and-place, underwater manipulators.
